title: Platform Dependency Risk

content.statement: If another company can change one rule and crush your economics, you do not fully own your business.
content.question: Which of your revenue streams or customer-acquisition channels could a single platform policy change wipe out overnight?
content.equation: PDR = Revenue Dependence × Policy Volatility × Switching Cost × Technical Dependence
sop: List every platform your business depends on for revenue, traffic, or payments (ads, marketplaces, app stores, payment processors, APIs).
Rate each platform 1-10 on Revenue Dependence, how much of your income runs through it (see calculation, page 6).

Rate each platform 1-10 on Policy Volatility, Switching Cost, and Technical Dependence, how often it changes rules, how hard it'd be to leave, and how deep it's wired into your systems (see calculation, page 6).
Multiply the four scores for your riskiest platform to get its score, and flag anything above 3,000 as urgent.
Start building one owned channel (email list, direct booking, owned app) this month to reduce dependence on your highest-risk platform.
content.explainer: You rent distribution from platforms. When their interests diverge from yours, they'll change the rules.
questions: Which of your revenue streams or customer-acquisition channels could a single platform policy change wipe out overnight?
If Meta, Google, Apple, or your payment processor changed one rule tomorrow, which part of your business breaks first?
What would it cost you in time and money to rebuild your customer list on a channel you fully own?

definitions.def: What it costs a customer or business — in money, time, or hassle — to move from one provider or platform to another. High switching costs lock you in.
Application Programming Interface — the connection that lets one software system plug into and pull data from another. Depending on a platform's API means relying on them to keep it open.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Revenue Dependence 8, Policy Volatility 6, Switching Cost 7, Technical Dependence 5 = 1,680. Higher scores mean one platform decision could crush your economics; lower scores mean you'd survive a policy change intact.
content.closing_statement: Rented distribution is borrowed time, not owned ground.

caption:
Principle Nº 46: Platform Dependency Risk

If another company can change one rule and crush your economics, you do not fully own your business. You rent distribution from platforms.

Ask yourself: Which of your revenue streams or customer-acquisition channels could a single platform policy change wipe out overnight?
